title: Process articulation — the ten-phase lifecycle .project/ blocks support
question: What is the full process lifecycle that the .project/ block substrate supports — from intention through verified completion — and where are the gaps that prevent the process from running unattended with integrity?
scope: Muni five-layer model × canonical engineering vocabulary
findings_summary:
The .project/ block substrate supports a ten-phase lifecycle for any unit of work at any scale (project, feature, story, task, gap closure, research investigation): (1) Intention/goal/articulation of done, (2) Research, (3) Initial spec, (4) Adversarial audit of spec + revision loop, (5) Implementation plan, (6) Adversarial audit of plan + revision loop, (7) Task implementation, (8) Adversarial audit of implementation, (9) Fix loop or verification pass, (10) Learning capture.

The lifecycle is fractal — the same ten phases apply whether the unit is a project, feature, story, or task. Blocks support nested application because every artifact kind has a finding registry, state machine, and cross-references. The traceability graph enables forward walk (project → feature → story → task → commit → file → decision → research → citation → ground truth), backward walk (commit → task → story → feature → project), and cross walk (decision → supersedes chain, research → informs → decision).

The process is operationally correct for a single human running the pipeline by hand with discipline. It is not yet mechanically enforceable against an unattended agent. The seven existing framework gaps (FGAP-001 through FGAP-007) plus ten new shortcomings surfaced in this articulation block mechanical enforcement: (1) goals block, (2) milestones block, (3) roadmap block, (4) verification criteria taxonomy on acceptance_criteria, (5) review target-kind ambiguity, (6) review iteration chain linking, (7) task git provenance (implemented_by commit SHAs), (8) verification.json schema depth, (9) lessons/feedback memory block, (10) postmortem block.
stale_conditions: .project/schemas/ set changes materially (new blocks added, lifecycle enums revised)
Any of FGAP-001 through FGAP-007 closed (changes what the substrate supports)
packages/pi-project/src/block-api.ts gains authorship stamping or transition validation

citations.label: FGAP-001 through FGAP-007 in issues block
